message:
fix(core): roll retained-autocommit flush back and reload memdb mirror on commit failure

Fixes a same-connection visibility bug in the retained-autocommit flush
path. Previously, when `txn.commit(cx)` failed mid-flush, the
connection cleared its cached state but left the pager txn hanging: the
pending writes were neither committed nor explicitly rolled back, and
the `MemDatabase` mirror still reflected the uncommitted inserts. A
subsequent `SELECT` on the same connection could observe rows that had
never actually been committed, and a reopen would see an empty table.

The new flow mirrors the normal autocommit cleanup:

1. Snapshot whether the txn had pending writes (`txn.has_pending_writes()`)
   before attempting the commit.
2. On `txn.commit(cx)` error, explicitly call `txn.rollback(cx)` and
   track whether the rollback itself succeeded.
3. If the txn had pending writes AND rollback succeeded, call
   `self.reload_memdb_from_pager(cx)?` so the in-memory mirror is
   rehydrated from the durable pager state. This is the same escape
   hatch used by the non-retained autocommit path.
4. Return the original commit error if rollback succeeded, otherwise
   return the rollback error (so the caller always sees the first
   failure that caused the abort).

The existing `clear_pending_memdb_direct_upserts()` and
`retained_autocommit_stmt_count.set(0)` cleanup still runs
unconditionally — only the pager/memdb reconciliation is new.

Test coverage
-------------
Extends the two failing-flush tests in `autocommit_txn_tests` to assert
that a same-connection `SELECT id FROM t ORDER BY id` after a failed
retained flush returns an empty result set. Both tests already
exercised the "reopen and verify" path; they now also catch the
stale-memdb regression without needing a reopen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
